name: Omoikane
description: Omoikane is a Shinto deity revered as the god of wisdom, intelligence, and counsel. His name literally translates to "serving one's thought." He is a prominent figure in Japanese mythology, particularly within the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the earliest chronicles of Japan. Omoikane's origins are somewhat obscure, but he is believed to have emerged as a divine entity embodying profound intellect and strategic thinking. He possesses the power to devise solutions to seemingly insurmountable problems and is often consulted by other deities, particularly Amaterasu, the sun goddess. His primary role is to provide guidance and strategic advice in critical situations. He is instrumental in the narrative of Amaterasu's seclusion in the Ama-no-Iwato (Rock Cave of Heaven), orchestrating the plan to lure her out using a grand celebration and the reflection of her light in the Yata no Kagami mirror. Omoikane's wisdom is considered essential for maintaining cosmic balance and resolving conflicts within the divine realm. His presence underscores the importance of intelligence and foresight in overcoming adversity. He is a symbol of strategic thinking and the power of intellect in the face of challenges, making him a revered figure in Shinto beliefs and practices. He is also linked to academic success and is sometimes invoked by students seeking wisdom and guidance in their studies. Omoikane's influence extends to various aspects of Japanese culture, emphasizing the value placed on knowledge and strategic planning.
subtitle: God of Wisdom and Counsel

keyMyths.title: Amaterasu's Seclusion
keyMyths.description: Omoikane devised the plan to lure Amaterasu out of the Ama-no-Iwato by organizing a lively celebration and using the Yata no Kagami mirror to reflect her light, piquing her curiosity. This plan successfully brought light back to the world.

keyMyths.title: Guidance to the Gods
keyMyths.description: Omoikane is frequently consulted by other deities when they face difficult situations, providing them with wise counsel and strategic solutions based on his vast knowledge and intellect. His advice is considered invaluable.

keyMyths.title: Divine Intellect
keyMyths.description: Omoikane's very existence embodies the concept of divine intellect and strategic thinking. His name signifies 'serving one's thought,' highlighting the importance of reason and foresight in the Shinto pantheon.
